Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial infection causing water-soaked lesions on pepper leaves and fruits.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruits; spots enlarge and become sunken.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.management: Apply copper-based bactericides; remove and destroy infected plant debris.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, avoid overhead irrigation, practice crop rotation.

Pepper__bell___healthy.description: The pepper plant is healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
Pepper__bell___healthy.symptoms: Vibrant green leaves and stems; normal growth.
Pepper__bell___healthy.management: Maintain balanced fertility and watering; monitor regularly for pests.
Pepper__bell___healthy.prevention: Practice crop rotation and maintain proper field sanitation.

Potato___healthy.description: Potato plant showing no disease or pest signs.
Potato___healthy.symptoms: Healthy foliage with green leaves and stems.
Potato___healthy.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes and rotate crops regularly.

Potato___Late_blight.description: A serious fungal disease caused by *Phytophthora infestans* affecting potatoes.
Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark, water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems; white fungal growth under leaves; tuber rot.
Potato___Late_blight.management: Apply appropriate fungicides (chlorothalonil, copper sprays); remove infected plant parts.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties; avoid overhead irrigation; practice crop rotation.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Fungal disease caused by *Alternaria solani* causing leaf spots.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Small, dark brown spots with concentric rings ('target spots') on leaves and stems.
Potato___Early_blight.management: Apply fungicides regularly; remove plant debris after harvest.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties; rotate crops; avoid planting near infected fields.

Tomato_healthy.description: Tomato plant showing no disease symptoms.
Tomato_healthy.symptoms: Healthy green leaves, stems, and fruit set.
Tomato_healthy.management: Maintain balanced nutrition and water management.
Tomato_healthy.prevention: Use quality seed; monitor regularly for pests.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial infection causing leaf spots and fruit blemishes.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked dark spots on leaves; spots on fruit surface.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.management: Use copper-based bactericides; remove infected foliage.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation; plant disease-free seed.

Tomato_Late_blight.description: Fungal disease causing leaf, stem, and fruit rot in tomatoes.
Tomato_Late_blight.symptoms: Dark brown lesions on leaves; white fuzzy mold under leaves; fruit decay.
Tomato_Late_blight.management: Apply fungicides; remove infected tissue promptly.
Tomato_Late_blight.prevention: Grow resistant varieties; avoid wet foliage; monitor humidity.

Tomato_Early_blight.description: Leaf spot disease caused by fungus *Alternaria* species.
Tomato_Early_blight.symptoms: Small, concentric ringed spots on older leaves; leaf yellowing and drop.
Tomato_Early_blight.management: Use fungicides as necessary; remove dead leaves.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.description: Fungal disease causing yellow spots and downy mold patches.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Yellow upper leaf spots; grayish to purple fuzz under leaves.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.management: Fungicide application; improve airflow in greenhouses.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Fungal infection producing leaf spots and defoliation.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark borders on leaf surfaces.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.description: Infestation by tiny spider mites causing stippling and webbing.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.symptoms: Yellow speckling, leaf curling, and fine spider webs on leaf undersides.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.management: Apply insecticidal soaps or neem oil; increase humidity.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.prevention: Regular scouting and biological control with predatory mites.

Tomato__Target_Spot.description: Fungal disease causing target-shaped leaf spots.
Tomato__Target_Spot.symptoms: Small dark spots with concentric rings on leaves.
Tomato__Target_Spot.management: Apply fungicides and remove affected leaves.

Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Viral infection leading to mottled leaf chlorosis.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mosaic pattern on leaves, leaf curling, stunted growth.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.management: Remove infected plants; control insects that spread virus.

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.description: Virus transmitted by whiteflies causing yellowing and curling.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.symptoms: Yellow, twisted leaves; stunted plant growth.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.management: Control whitefly populations with insecticides and traps.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-free seedlings; cover crops with netting.
